requirements.summary: Add tweetnacl as a frontend dependency and ensure TypeScript can import it during production builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend dependencies include `tweetnacl` (listed under dependencies in the frontend package manifest).
A production frontend build succeeds with a simple `import nacl from "tweetnacl"` usage (no missing-module errors).

file_operations.description: Create or update the frontend npm package manifest to include `tweetnacl` under `dependencies` so TypeScript/React code can import it. If a manifest already exists elsewhere in the frontend workspace, modify that instead and keep this file in sync with the project’s actual package root.

Update the frontend lockfile (npm) to reflect adding `tweetnacl`, if this project uses npm. If the project uses a different package manager, update the applicable lockfile instead and avoid creating conflicting lockfiles.

Update the frontend lockfile (pnpm) to reflect adding `tweetnacl`, if this project uses pnpm. If the project uses a different package manager, update the applicable lockfile instead and avoid creating conflicting lockfiles.

Update the frontend lockfile (Yarn) to reflect adding `tweetnacl`, if this project uses Yarn. If the project uses a different package manager, update the applicable lockfile instead and avoid creating conflicting lockfiles.
Add a small TypeScript module that imports `tweetnacl` (e.g., default import) and exports a tiny helper/constant so the dependency is exercised by the compiler during builds.

Import the `tweetnacl` smoke-test module (or directly import `tweetnacl`) in a non-immutable file to ensure a production build includes and validates module resolution, without changing any Ed25519 key generation logic or touching immutable hook paths.
